title: NOC 64409 – Other customer and information services representatives | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 64409 is a versatile and essential category covering specialized information representatives who act as the "knowledge hub" for various Canadian organizations. From handling complex inquiries at public libraries and social service agencies to managing customer relations in corporate settings, these TEER 4 professionals ensure the public can navigate complex systems and access vital information. For immigration, this role is a strong entry point for candidates with high language proficiency and administrative backgrounds, offering a stable career path with clear progression into specialized coordination or management.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group644 – Customer and information services representativesMinor Group6440 – Customer and information services representativesNOC Code64409

duties: Answer inquiries and provide information regarding an organization's products, services, and policies.
Process requests for information and provide relevant materials or referrals to other departments.
Maintain records of inquiries and responses using specialized database software.
Assist the public in completing forms or navigating online service portals.
Resolve customer complaints and document feedback for service improvement.
Perform basic administrative tasks such as filing, data entry, and scheduling appointments.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Some college or university education may be preferred depending on the complexity of the information being provided (e.g., legal or medical information).
On-the-job training is typically provided by the employer.
Proficiency in English or French is mandatory; bilingualism is a major asset in federal or national organizations.
Strong digital literacy and experience with Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software are highly beneficial.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. Projections indicate that as the "Information Economy" expands, the role of human representatives in filtering and explaining complex data will remain critical, even with the rise of AI-driven support tools.
labour_market_demand: The demand for information services representatives is Stable to Good across Canada. As organizations in the public and private sectors prioritize "Customer Experience" (CX), the need for skilled representatives to manage multi-channel inquiries (phone, email, chat, and in-person) has increased. Demand is particularly consistent in government-heavy regions like Ottawa (Ontario) and in major urban centers where large corporate headquarters are located.
Professional Growth: Excellent stepping stone to becoming a Customer Service Supervisor (NOC 62024) or Office Manager (NOC 12100).
Workplace Diversity: Opportunity to work in varied environments, from academic institutions to international corporations.
In-Demand Communication Skills: Highly valued for candidates who can bridge the gap between complex organizational data and the general public.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: A popular pathway for those working in the service sector (requires 9 months of work in BC).
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track for those already working in Alberta in this category with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for TEER 4 workers with job offers in the Atlantic provinces.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Targeted at specific service roles that support Ontario’s labor market.
